Class: EvVehicles

bemap.EvVehicles(context, optionsopt)

new EvVehicles(context, optionsopt)

BeNomad BeMap JavaScript API - EvVehicles service (v1.5) Promise-based wrapper around the v1.1 vehicle catalogue endpoints: - `GET service/vehicle/1.1/getbrands` → `{brands: [{id, label}, ...]}` - `GET service/vehicle/1.1/findvehicles` → `{vehicles: [VehicleInfo, ...]}` **Why v1.1 (not the lightweight `evsmartrouting/vehicles*` endpoints).** v1.1 returns structured plaintext fields — brandName, name, year, variant, batteryName (kWh), chargerPowerDC (kW), chargerPowerAcThreePhases (kW), connectorTypes[], wltp, consumptionInWhPerKm, dimensions — same shape the Flutter SDK consumes via `bemap-flutter-api/lib/service/vehicle/model/vehicle.dart`. No regex parsing of pre-formatted titles, no AES decryption of `encDatasheet`. Auth roles: `ROLE_VEHICLE` (+ `ROLE_VEHICLE_DATASHEET` for `enableDatasheet: true`). `bemap.EvSmartRouting.getBrands()` / `.getVehicles()` keep their existing lightweight `evsmartrouting/vehicles*` endpoints for backward compat — the two services serve different use cases.
Parameters:
Name Type Attributes Description
context bemap.Context
options Object <optional>
Since:
  • 1.5.0
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev/bemap-ev-vehicles.js, line 31

Extends

  • bemap.ServiceV2

Methods

brandLogo(brandId, optionsopt) → {Promise.<{brandId: String, pictureFileFormat: Object, logo: String}>}

Fetch the JSON-wrapped logo description for an EV brand (includes the base64-encoded image bytes and the picture format). Wraps `GET service/vehicle/1.1/getbrandlogo`. For embedding the logo directly in `` markup, prefer `getBrandLogoUrl()` — it returns the URL of the binary endpoint.
Parameters:
Name Type Attributes Description
brandId String
options Object <optional>
Properties
Name Type Attributes Description
signal AbortSignal <optional>
Since:
  • 1.5.0
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev/bemap-ev-vehicles.js, line 192
Returns:
`logo` is base64-encoded image bytes.
Type
Promise.<{brandId: String, pictureFileFormat: Object, logo: String}>

brands(optionsopt) → {Promise.<Array.<bemap.EvBrand>>}

List EV brands the current user has access to.
Parameters:
Name Type Attributes Description
options Object <optional>
Properties
Name Type Attributes Description
signal AbortSignal <optional>
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev/bemap-ev-vehicles.js, line 43
Returns:
Sorted by label for deterministic UI.
Type
Promise.<Array.<bemap.EvBrand>>

get(vehicleKey, optionsopt) → {Promise.<bemap.EvVehicle>}

Fetch one vehicle by its UUID key.
Parameters:
Name Type Attributes Description
vehicleKey String
options Object <optional>
Properties
Name Type Attributes Default Description
enableDatasheet Boolean <optional>
 false
signal AbortSignal <optional>
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev/bemap-ev-vehicles.js, line 97
Returns:
Rejects with `bemap.Error.VEHICLE_NOT_FOUND` when no match.
Type
Promise.<bemap.EvVehicle>

getBrandLogoUrl(brandId) → {String}

Build the URL of the binary brand-logo endpoint (`GET service/vehicle/1.1/getbrandlogolite`) — returns raw image bytes with the correct `Content-Type`. Customers fetch this with their own auth (Basic via `Authorization` header) and turn the response into a `Blob` URL for ``.
Parameters:
Name Type Description
brandId String
Since:
  • 1.5.0
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev/bemap-ev-vehicles.js, line 216
Returns:
Full URL including the Context's base URL.
Type
String

getPictureUrl(vehicleKey) → {String}

Build the URL of the vehicle-photo endpoint (`GET service/vehicle/picture/1.0/getone?vehicleUuidKey=`). Used by customer UIs (e.g. `evmove5`'s car-select screen) to render vehicle thumbnails. Customers fetch with their own auth.
Parameters:
Name Type Description
vehicleKey String
Since:
  • 1.5.0
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev/bemap-ev-vehicles.js, line 232
Returns:
Type
String

levels(filters) → {Promise.<Array.<String>>}

Walk the catalogue progressively, one attribute at a time — used by cascading "brand → name → variant → battery → DC power → AC power" UI flows (as in the `evmove5` car-select screen). Wraps `GET service/vehicle/1.1/getlevelvehicleinfo`. The `level` argument names which attribute the server returns. Each deeper level filters by the levels already chosen. Levels: `'NAME'` · `'VARIANT'` · `'YEAR'` · `'BATTERY_NAME'` · `'CHARGE_POWER_DC'` · `'CHARGE_POWER_AC'` · `'MOTOR_TYPE'`.
Parameters:
Name Type Description
filters Object
Properties
Name Type Attributes Description
level String **R** One of the level names above.
brandId String **R**
name String <optional>
variant String <optional>
year String <optional>
batteryName String <optional>
motorType String <optional>
chargerPowerDC Number <optional>
chargerPowerAC Number <optional>
signal AbortSignal <optional>
Since:
  • 1.5.0
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev/bemap-ev-vehicles.js, line 147
Returns:
Type
Promise.<Array.<String>>

list(filtersopt) → {Promise.<Array.<bemap.EvVehicle>>}

List vehicles, optionally filtered by brand or by an exact vehicle key.
Parameters:
Name Type Attributes Description
filters Object <optional>
Properties
Name Type Attributes Default Description
brandId String <optional>
 Restrict to one brand.
vehicle String <optional>
 Restrict to one vehicle key (returns 0 or 1 entries).
variant String <optional>
 Restrict to one variant (v1.1).
enableDatasheet Boolean <optional>
 false Include the encrypted datasheet blob.
signal AbortSignal <optional>
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/ev/bemap-ev-vehicles.js, line 72
Returns:
Type
Promise.<Array.<bemap.EvVehicle>>

toRoutingProfile(vehicleKey, optionsopt) → {Promise.<bemap.RoutingVehicleProfile>}

Convenience: fetch a vehicle and return a `RoutingVehicleProfile` ready to plug into `bemap.RoutingV2.calculate()` or `bemap.EvSmartRouting.calculate()`.
Parameters:
Name Type Attributes Description
vehicleKey String
options Object <optional>
Properties
Name Type Attributes Description
signal AbortSignal <optional>

Type
Promise.<bemap.RoutingVehicleProfile>
